items.title: Zero units for 97110
items.detail: Therapeutic Exercise has 3s recorded but 0 billable units under the 8-minute rule.

items.title: Capture Baseline Shoulder ROM and Pain
items.detail:
Document active and/or passive range of motion (degrees) for shoulder flexion, abduction, and external rotation, along with current pain level (0-10 scale) before beginning therapeutic exercise.

AI-generated suggestions require clinician review before use.

items.title: Document Specific Exercise Details
items.detail:
Record which shoulder exercises were performed, including sets, repetitions, resistance level, and patient tolerance/response during the session.

AI-generated suggestions require clinician review before use.

items.title: Clarify Shoulder Pain Onset and Aggravating Factors
items.detail:
Consider documenting when the shoulder pain began, what activities aggravate or relieve it, and any prior treatment history to establish medical necessity.

AI-generated suggestions require clinician review before use.

latest_analysis.summary: Conversation segment reviewed: therapeutic exercise for shoulder pain
possible_clinical_impressions: Shoulder pain / possible range of motion limitation
possible_diagnoses: Shoulder pain / possible range of motion limitation

cpt_suggestions.label: therapeutic_exercise
cpt_suggestions.descriptor: Application of therapeutic procedures to one or more areas, each 15 minutes, utilizing structured exercises designed to improve, manage, or restore physiological range of motion, physical strength, muscle endurance, and soft tissue flexibility.
documentation_requirements: Objective baseline metrics for strength (e.g., manual muscle testing grades) and range of motion measured via goniometry.
Clear descriptions of specific exercises performed, including sets, repetitions, resistance levels, and anatomical areas targeted.
Explicit documentation of skilled clinical intervention, such as verbal cues for form correction, manual assistance, or physiological monitoring.
Direct correlation between the selected exercises and the functional progression goals outlined in the patient care plan.

billing_caveats.time_system: CMS 8-Minute Rule compliant. Total cumulative timed minutes across all direct treatments determine total billable units per session.

payer_specific_rules.medicare_part_b: Requires direct, one-on-one visual and manual contact by a licensed clinician or certified assistant.
payer_specific_rules.commercial_payers: Some non-Medicare lines follow the Substantial Portion Method (15-minute midpoint rules) rather than cumulative rounding.

ncci_conflict_risks: Bundled with 97140 (Manual Therapy) when executed on identical anatomical zones unless distinct time intervals and separate goals are validated.
Subject to overutilization audits if billed consistently as the sole intervention across multi-week cycles without functional progression.

cpt_suggestions.reason: Indicated when a patient presents with demonstrable deficits in joint mobility, active or passive range of motion, muscular weakness, or diminished structural endurance resulting from injury, surgery, or chronic degenerative disease processes.

soap_update.assessment: Possible clinical impressions to review: Shoulder pain / possible range of motion limitation.
soap_update.plan: Review therapy plan, skilled minutes, and documentation support for detected treatment themes. Documentation gaps: Baseline manual muscle testing (MMT) was not documented before therapeutic exercise.; Suggested fix: document baseline MMT grades for involved muscles before skilled exercise..

billing_hints: Documented 'therapeutic exercise' may support Therapeutic Exercise (97110).
latest_analysis.disclaimer: Rules-based detection requires clinician review before billing.

subjective.duration: Not documented in session. Onset, aggravating factors, and relieving factors not specified in available transcript.

objective.observation_notes: Therapeutic exercise performed for right shoulder. Patient engaged in skilled therapeutic exercise intervention. Specific exercise details, sets, repetitions, resistance level, and patient tolerance during exercise not documented in available transcript.
objective.range_of_motion: Not documented in session. Baseline active and passive range of motion measurements for shoulder flexion, abduction, and external rotation not recorded.

assessment.diagnosis_summary: Patient presents with right shoulder pain. Therapeutic exercise was initiated as skilled intervention. However, baseline clinical measurements including pain severity (0-10 scale), shoulder range of motion (flexion, abduction, external rotation in degrees), and manual muscle testing grades were not documented prior to intervention. These baseline measurements are essential to establish medical necessity, quantify functional limitations, and track progress toward rehabilitation goals. Patient response to today's therapeutic exercise and functional progress cannot be fully assessed without baseline and post-intervention measurements. Continued skilled therapy may be warranted pending documentation of baseline functional status and treatment response. Documentation gaps to address: Baseline manual muscle testing (MMT) was not documented before therapeutic exercise..

plan.follow_up_plan: Recommend documenting baseline shoulder range of motion (flexion, abduction, external rotation), pain level (0-10 scale), and manual muscle testing grades at next session prior to therapeutic exercise. Continue therapeutic exercise as tolerated. Specific home exercise program, frequency of follow-up visits, activity modifications, and precautions not documented in available transcript. Clinician to establish clear visit frequency, duration of care, and home exercise prescription at next encounter. Suggested fix: document baseline MMT grades for involved muscles before skilled exercise.

compliance_gaps: Baseline manual muscle testing (MMT) was not documented before therapeutic exercise.
Suggested fix: document baseline MMT grades for involved muscles before skilled exercise.

patient_summary.summary:
Hi Jane! Today we worked on therapeutic exercises to help with your shoulder pain. We performed exercises to strengthen and improve your shoulder function. To help track your progress better, at your next visit we'll measure your shoulder movement and document your pain level so we can see exactly how much you're improving. Keep up with your exercises, and let us know if you have any questions about what to do at home!

AI-generated suggestions require clinician review before use.
